analysis.background: Andes virus (ANDV) is a hantavirus capable of human-to-human transmission and causes severe hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome.
analysis.main_directions: The review summarizes epidemiology, clinical features, transmission routes, and public health responses using the 2026 MV Hondius cruise ship outbreak as a case study.
analysis.current_state: By May 27, 13 cases (11 confirmed, 2 probable) and 3 deaths were reported; genomic data showed minimal variation, indicating onboard human-to-human spread with an Rₑ of 0.7, driven by age, shared cabins, and close contact.
analysis.gaps: Early detection was delayed, and the small outbreak size limits generalizability of transmission dynamics and control measures.
analysis.future_research: Future work should focus on routine travel history assessment, enhanced surveillance for suspected cases, rapid diagnostics, and preparedness for importation of low‑transmissibility pathogens in high‑density settings.

analysis.summary_en: Background: Andes virus (ANDV) is a hantavirus capable of human-to-human transmission and causes severe hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome. Main directions: The review summarizes epidemiology, clinical features, transmission routes, and public health responses using the 2026 MV Hondius cruise ship outbreak as a case study. Current state: By May 27, 13 cases (11 confirmed, 2 probable) and 3 deaths were reported; genomic data showed minimal variation, indicating onboard human-to-human spread with an Rₑ of 0.7, driven by age, shared cabins, and close contact. Gaps: Early detection was delayed, and the small outbreak size limits generalizability of transmission dynamics and control measures. Future research: Future work should focus on routine travel history assessment, enhanced surveillance for suspected cases, rapid diagnostics, and preparedness for importation of low‑transmissibility pathogens in high‑density settings.

papers.title_zh: 2026年邮轮相关安第斯病毒汉坦病毒爆发与公共卫生应对

analysis_zh.background: 安第斯病毒(安第斯病毒)是一种汉坦病毒,具有人传人能力并可导致严重的汉坦病毒心肺综合征。
analysis_zh.main_directions: 该综述以2026年MV Hondius邮轮疫情为案例,总结了流行病学、临床特征、传播途径及公共卫生应对措施。
analysis_zh.current_state: 截至5月27日,报告13例病例(11例确诊,2例疑似)和3例死亡;基因组数据显示变异极小,表明船内人传人,Re值为0.7,传播驱动因素包括年龄、共用舱室和密切接触。
analysis_zh.gaps: 早期检测延迟,且小规模疫情限制了传播动态和控制措施的普遍适用性。
analysis_zh.future_research: 未来研究应聚焦于常规旅行史评估、疑似病例的加强监测、快速诊断技术,以及高密度环境中对低传播性病原体输入的应对准备。

papers.summary_zh: 背景：安第斯病毒(安第斯病毒)是一种汉坦病毒,具有人传人能力并可导致严重的汉坦病毒心肺综合征。 主要方向：该综述以2026年MV Hondius邮轮疫情为案例,总结了流行病学、临床特征、传播途径及公共卫生应对措施。 研究现状：截至5月27日,报告13例病例(11例确诊,2例疑似)和3例死亡;基因组数据显示变异极小,表明船内人传人,Re值为0.7,传播驱动因素包括年龄… 不足：早期检测延迟,且小规模疫情限制了传播动态和控制措施的普遍适用性。 后续研究：未来研究应聚焦于常规旅行史评估、疑似病例的加强监测、快速诊断技术,以及高密度环境中对低传播性病原体输入的应对准备。

papers.title: Spectrum of Gastrointestinal, Hepatobiliary, and Pancreatic Involvement in Hantavirus Infection: A Narrative Review.
papers.abstract: Hantavirus infection is a zoonotic, rodent-transmitted viral disease that presents with two distinct clinical syndromes: hemorrhagic fever with renal failure (HFRS) and hantavirus pulmonary syndrome (HPS). The 2026 cruise ship hantavirus outbreak has once again brought this infrequent disease into the limelight. Gastrointestinal (GI), hepatobiliary, and pancreatic manifestations, though frequent, have garnered less attention in patients with hantavirus infections. This narrative review summarizes the available literature on GI and hepatopancreatic involvement in hantavirus infection. A comprehensive review of the literature published between 2000 and May 2026 was conducted using PubMed. Eligible studies included observational studies, systematic reviews, meta-analyses, case series, and case reports describing GI, hepatic, biliary, or pancreatic manifestations in human hantavirus infection. The literature review identified GI symptoms among the earliest and most frequent prodromal manifestations of hantavirus disease. Nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, and diarrhea often precede organ-specific complications by several days. GI bleeding develops as a component of hemorrhagic syndrome. Hepatic involvement most frequently manifests as transient elevations in aminotransferase levels and, less frequently, as hyperbilirubinemia. Gallbladder wall thickening, acalculous cholecystitis, instances of hantavirus-triggered autoimmune hepatitis, and acute pancreatitis have also been reported. Current management strategies are primarily supportive, including renal replacement therapy for severe renal injury and ventilatory support and extracorporeal membrane oxygenation for severe pulmonary disease. Antiviral therapies such as ribavirin and favipiravir have shown inconsistent or limited clinical benefits. Limited evidence links early and prominent GI symptoms and transaminitis with more severe disease and a worse prognosis. A high index of suspicion in patients with GI symptoms may facilitate earlier diagnosis and allow risk stratification. However, the current evidence is sparse, mostly derived from case reports, case series, and small retrospective studies, which limits our understanding of GI and hepatobiliary-pancreatic manifestations in patients with hantavirus infections. Large prospective studies are needed to clarify the mechanisms, incidence, and prognostic significance of these manifestations.

analysis.background: Hantavirus infection is a rodent‑borne zoonosis causing hemorrhagic fever with renal failure and pulmonary syndrome, and a recent cruise‑ship outbreak has renewed focus on its clinical spectrum.
analysis.main_directions: GI, hepatobiliary, and pancreatic manifestations—early prodromal GI symptoms, transaminitis, gallbladder changes, pancreatitis—are frequent but under‑studied; supportive care remains mainstay, antivirals show limited benefit.
analysis.current_state: Evidence suggests early GI and liver enzyme elevations may predict severe disease, yet data are mainly case reports and small series.
analysis.gaps: Sparse, heterogeneous data limit understanding of incidence, mechanisms, and prognostic value; lack of prospective studies.
analysis.future_research: Large prospective cohorts and mechanistic studies are needed to quantify prevalence, clarify pathophysiology, and evaluate targeted therapies.

analysis.summary_en: Background: Hantavirus infection, a rodent‑borne zoonosis, causes hemorrhagic fever with renal failure and pulmonary syndrome; a recent cruise‑ship outbreak has highlighted its clinical spectrum. Main directions: GI, hepatobiliary, and pancreatic involvement—early prodromal GI symptoms, transaminitis, gallbladder changes, pancreatitis—are frequent but under‑studied; supportive care remains mainstay, antivirals show limited benefit. Current state: Evidence suggests early GI and liver enzyme elevations may predict severe disease, yet data are mainly case reports and small series. Gaps: Sparse, heterogeneous data limit understanding of incidence, mechanisms, and prognostic value; lack of prospective studies. Future research: Large prospective cohorts and mechanistic studies are needed to quantify prevalence, clarify pathophysiology, and evaluate targeted therapies.

papers.title_zh: 汉坦病毒感染的胃肠、肝胆及胰腺受累范围:叙述性综述

analysis_zh.background: 汉坦病毒感染是一种由啮齿动物传播的自然疫源性疾病,可导致肾综合征出血热和汉坦病毒肺综合征,最近一艘游轮上的疫情暴发重新引发了对其临床谱的关注。
analysis_zh.main_directions: 胃肠道、肝胆和胰腺表现——早期前驱胃肠道症状、转氨酶升高、胆囊改变、胰腺炎——较为常见但研究不足;支持治疗仍是主要手段,抗病毒药物效果有限。
analysis_zh.current_state: 现有证据表明早期胃肠道和肝酶升高可能预示严重疾病,但数据主要来自病例报告和小样本研究。
analysis_zh.gaps: 数据稀少且异质性高,限制了对发病率、机制和预后价值的理解;缺乏前瞻性研究。
analysis_zh.future_research: 需要大规模前瞻性队列和机制研究以量化患病率、阐明发病机制并评估靶向治疗。

papers.summary_zh: 背景：汉坦病毒感染是一种由啮齿动物传播的自然疫源性疾病,可导致肾综合征出血热和汉坦病毒肺综合征,最近一艘游轮上的疫情暴发重新引发了对其临床谱的关注。 主要方向：胃肠道、肝胆和胰腺表现——早期前驱胃肠道症状、转氨酶升高、胆囊改变、胰腺炎——较为常见但研究不足;支持治疗仍是主要手段,抗病毒药物效果有限。 研究现状：现有证据表明早期胃肠道和肝酶升高可能预示严重疾病,但数据主要来自病例报告和小样本研究。 不足：数据稀少且异质性高,限制了对发病率、机制和预后价值的理解;缺乏前瞻性研究。 后续研究：需要大规模前瞻性队列和机制研究以量化患病率、阐明发病机制并评估靶向治疗。
